ucs.title: [LLM] Molerats / Gaza Cybergang MSI delivery chain from cloud-hosted spearphishing lure
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Molerats infection chain: politically-themed spearphishing link (T1566.002) clicked from Office/Outlook/browser, dropping or invoking msiexec.exe to silently install a remote MSI hosted on Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive or MediaFire. This sequence is a fingerprint of Molerats SharpStage / DropBook / MoleNet campaigns documented by Cybereason (Dec 2020) and Kaspersky (Apr 2019).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=msiexec.exe AND (Processes.process="*dropbox.com*" OR Processes.process="*dl.dropboxusercontent*" OR Processes.process="*drive.google.com*" OR Processes.process="*googleusercontent*" OR Processes.process="*onedrive.live.com*" OR Processes.process="*1drv.ms*" OR Processes.process="*mediafire.com*" OR Processes.process="*bit.ly*") AND (Processes.process="*/q*" OR Processes.process="*/quiet*" OR Processes.process="*/qn*" OR Processes.process="*/qb*") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let phishingParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe"]);
let cloudHosts = @"(?i)https?://[^\s""]*(dropbox\.com|dl\.dropboxusercontent|drive\.google\.com|googleusercontent|onedrive\.live\.com|1drv\.ms|mediafire\.com|bit\.ly|tinyurl\.com)";
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex cloudHosts
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/q","/quiet","/qn","/qb")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/i","-i")
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (phishingParents)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| join kind=leftouter (
    UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | project ClickTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn, Url, NetworkMessageId
) on $left.AccountName == $right.AccountUpn
| where isnull(ClickTime) or datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, ClickTime) between (0 .. 60)
ucs.rationale: Molerats almost exclusively stages its loaders (SharpStage, DropBook, MoleNet, Spark) on Dropbox / Google Drive and pulls them via msiexec /i /q after the victim opens an Arabic-language political lure. The triple constraint (msiexec + cloud-storage URL + Office/script/browser parent) collapses generic msiexec noise to near-zero in enterprise environments — legitimate IT tooling neither hosts MSIs

ucs.title: [LLM] Molerats SharpStage/DropBook backdoor: scripted C2 to Dropbox API + browser credential theft
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation half of the Molerats kill chain: scripted backdoors (SharpStage/DropBook in Python-compiled and PowerShell variants) that abuse the Dropbox/Google Drive/Facebook Graph APIs for C2, persist via Run keys or scheduled tasks, and steal Chrome/Edge Login Data. Joining script-interpreter Dropbox-API beacons with same-host access to browser credential stores fingerprints Gaza Cybergang activity vs. routine cloud usage.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="api.dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="content.dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.googleapis.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="graph.facebook.com") AND All_Traffic.app IN ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","python.exe","pythonw.exe") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.user as user All_Traffic.app as proc All_Traffic.dest as c2dest | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*logins.json*") AND Filesystem.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","python.exe","pythonw.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path | rename Filesystem.dest as host Filesystem.process_name as cred_proc Filesystem.file_path as cred_file] | table host user proc c2dest cred_proc cred_file count
ucs.kql:
let scriptHosts = dynamic(["powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","python.exe","pythonw.exe"]);
let cloudC2 = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","www.googleapis.com/drive","graph.facebook.com")
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (scriptHosts)
| project C2Time=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, C2Proc=InitiatingProcessFileName, C2Cmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let credAccess = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Google\Chrome\User Data", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles")
| where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Login Data-journal","logins.json","key4.db","Cookies")
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (scriptHosts) or InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("powershell","wscript","python")
| project CredTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, FolderPath, FileName, CredProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, CredCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
cloudC2
| join kind=inner credAccess on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('hour', C2Time, CredTime)) <= 24
| project DeviceName, C2Time, CredTime, RemoteUrl, C2Proc, C2Cmd, CredProc, FolderPath, FileName
ucs.rationale: The dual signal — a scripting host (powershell/wscript/python) talking to Dropbox/Google Drive/Facebook Graph APIs AND the same scripting host touching Chrome/Edge/Firefox credential stores within 24h — is the operational fingerprint of Gaza Cybergang's SharpStage and DropBook backdoors as reported by Cybereason and Kaspersky. Legitimate Dropbox/Drive desktop clients do not run as powershell.exe a
